rationale: The cited 2000 paper is highly method-relevant: it develops store-level scanner models with lead/lag promotion dynamics (pre/postpromotion dips), while the 2002 citing paper estimates store-level dynamic adjustment and long-term promotion effects using impulse-response decomposition. Given this direct overlap in constructs (promotion persistence/dips) and empirical level (store-level scanner data), the citation is plausibly foundational or methodological rather than an incidental mention.
